content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (post iter-026)

## Closest-to-completion (prioritize)
1. **K1 η-collapse `pushforward_eta_appIso_collapse` (TensorObjSubstrate.lean L4182) — NEARLY CLOSED.**
   Residual is one `rfl`-shaped presheaf-level lemma + 2 rewrites. Action: add
   `pushforwardPushforwardAdj_unit_app_app_apply : … := rfl` (presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
   `pushforwardPushforwardAdj_unit_app_val_app`), orientation per `PresheafInternalHom.lean:442`
   (`adj : F ⊣ G`, `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, `ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S` — the unit's `M` is in the SOURCE of the LEFT
   pushforward; that orientation friction is what stalled the prover's first try). Then close L4182 with
   `erw [<that lemma>]; erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]; rw [map_one]`. Single short prover lane.

2. **Terminal cocycle A `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (TensorObjInverse.lean L239) — BLUEPRINT-FIRST.**
   Do NOT re-dispatch a plain prover (it will re-hit the same wall — see Known Blockers). The closure
   needs two NEW helper lemmas that exist only as prose in `rem:dual_discharges_inverse`
   (lvb-inverse026 major): (A) further-restriction compatibility of `tensorObj_restrict_iso` /
   `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` / `pullbackUnitIso`; (B) unit self-duality eval collapse
   `tensorObjIsoOfIso t (dualIsoOfIso t)⁻¹ ≫ tensorObj_unit_iso = tensorObj_unit_iso`. Action: dispatch
   blueprint-writer to author `\lean{}` target blocks for (A) and (B) under `rem:dual_discharges_inverse`;
   then a prover lane on those two helpers; then A closes by lift-via-(A) + `simp [..._trans/_refl]` +
   collapse-via-(B).

## CHURNING / structural — must not re-attempt as-is
3. **K1 μ-collapse `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse` (L4287) — mate route is CIRCULAR (confirmed iter-026).**
   `unit_app_tensor_comp_map_δ hadj'` / `unit_app_unit_comp_map_η hadj'` both need `hadj'.IsMonoidal` =
   the very `hmon` these lemmas build. **Do NOT route μ through the mate calculus.** The genuine residual
   is the BARE tensorator comparison `μ(rightAdjointLaxMonoidal hadj') = μ(presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal)`
   on `Gβ A, Gβ B`, proved DIRECTLY sectionwise on `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` pure tensors (mirror the
   PROVED `pushforwardComp_lax_μ`, L2197), threading the D3′ μ-helper family by `erw`; `tensor_ext` won't
   fire (`Gβ.obj(A⊗B)` is a pushforward of a tensor, not syntactic). pc026 already flagged a
   **mathlib-analogist** consult on the `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`-threading seam — dispatch it (or
   effort-break the μ-collapse into LHS-`δGβ`-collapse / RHS-`δpullback=μ`-collapse halves) before any
   prover lane. Multi-hundred LOC; the single hardest remaining node.

## Stale-comment cleanup (lean-auditor iter026: 11 major, all stale — refactor lane)
A whole-tree comment-only refactor pass (the auditor confirms NO live code defect; these mislead the
planner/next prover):
- `DualInverse.lean` module docstring (L18-48), L198-201, `dual_isLocallyTrivial` planner block (L236-273):
  still say "PARTIAL / one sorry remains / TRANSITIVELY PARTIAL / Step-4 sorry at ~L546" — all now CLOSED
  (file + `SliceTransport.lean` are sorry-free, axiom-clean).
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` L46 "SOLE in-file sorry is hmon" (now TWO explicit sorries in private collapse
  lemmas); L161-162 resident list lists `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (MOVED to TensorObjInverse.lean) and
  marks `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial` sorry-bodied (its body is closed); L3452/L3513 "typed sorry
  retained" (decl closed).
- `TensorObjInverse.lean` L140-142 "both bridges remain" (B closed iter-026; A is the only residual).
- `Vestigial.lean` L15 reports the `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` sorry (closed iter-237).
Reports: `task_results/lean-auditor-iter026.md`, `…-dualinverse026.md`.

## Coverage debt (unmatched = 104, was 110)
`archon dag-query unmatched` lists 104 `lean_aux` nodes with no blueprint entry (all `proved:true,
has_sorry:false` — proved helpers). Connector `homOfLocalCompat_restrictFunctor_map` IS matched (node
`lem:hom_of_local_compat_restrict`). No NEW unmatched decls were introduced this iter (the prover's
`key` is an in-proof `have`, not a top-level decl; the trialled unit-value helper was removed). The 104
are the standing backlog of helper lemmas from prior iters — the planner should chip at these with
blueprint stubs when bandwidth allows; none gate the seeds.

## Reusable proof patterns
- `let` (not `have`) for adjunction terms when the statement signature `let`s zeta-unfold a
  `leftAdjointUniq`/`pushforwardPushforwardAdj` into the goal — opaque `have` breaks `rw`/`erw`
  key-matching. (Same family as the iter-025 `haveI→letI` carrier-diamond fix.)
- restrictFunctor glued-morphism value (connector): reconstruct gluing internals defeq + `change` to
  `g`-form + morphism-level `key` collapsing the eqToHom-conjugation via `eqToHom_comp_iff` +
  `exact`-matched `naturality`.

## Dispatch hygiene (process)
The connector lane was silently dropped for 3 iters because plan-validate removes objectives for files
with zero sorries. When an objective is "prove a NOT-YET-EXISTING declaration", scaffold the stub
(lean-scaffolder) in the SAME plan phase so the lane actually dispatches — as done this iter.
